page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8y.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on the custom 64-bit Arm-based Alibaba Yitian710 processor, operating at a clock speed of 2.75 GHz. The processor architecture includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 64 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core allocation. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the ecs.c8y.2xlarge in the average tier for most general compute and memory operations. The Yitian710 processor achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1820.08 and a multi-core score of 14489.92. Memory bandwidth performance is measured at 820.26 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, and 96.36 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-threaded gzip compression reaches 28.55 GB/sec, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs at 82.26 GB/sec. However, cryptographic performance is relatively weak, with a Passmark encryption score of 1320.86.

Given its dedicated Arm-based cores and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance delivers a cost-efficient profile for scale-out compute workloads that do not require x86 instruction sets. It is well-suited for static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 12.71 GB/sec, and high-throughput key-value stores, as demonstrated by a Redis SET performance of 13.18 million operations per second. It can also handle lightweight machine learning tasks, such as prompt processing for small 135M parameter models, though it is poorly suited for larger 7B parameter model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8y.2xlarge is an Arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores of an Alibaba Yitian710 processor running at 2.75 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14489.92 and a cached read memory bandwidth of 820.26 GB/sec. While cryptographic performance is weak, the instance excels in high-throughput scenarios, delivering 13.18 million Redis SET operations per second and 12.71 GB/sec in static web serving. It represents a resource-dense, cost-efficient option for scale-out workloads, web serving, and lightweight Arm-native applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8y.2xlarge is an Arm64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated Yitian710 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced performance for web serving and Redis workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8y.2xlarge is an Arm64 compute-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Yitian710 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An Arm64 compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated physical cores for efficient web serving and high-throughput key-value store workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated physical Arm64 cores on the Alibaba Yitian710 processor running at 2.75 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM hypervisor integration
Strong performance in static web serving and Redis key-value operations
Optimized for Arm-native scale-out workloads and lightweight containerized applications
